skill.name: hyperframes-registry
skill.description: Install and wire HyperFrames registry blocks and components into video compositions, including add commands, install paths, host HTML wiring, and registry discovery.
skill.summary: Wire HyperFrames registry blocks and components into existing compositions.
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many backtick and weak-crypto patterns, but review found markdown code formatting, CLI examples, HTML snippets, and table text rather than hidden execution or cryptographic code. The skill documents user-initiated HyperFrames commands, local project file writes, and known GitHub/CDN URLs; no prompt injection, credential access, or exfiltration was found.

low_findings.title: User-Initiated CLI Commands and Local Writes
low_findings.description: The skill documents HyperFrames commands that install registry items, lint projects, preview compositions, and write files under configured project paths. This is intended workflow guidance, not hidden execution, but users should run it only inside trusted projects.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands and file writes are explicit documentation examples. I found no evidence that the skill executes commands automatically or hides shell behavior.

low_findings.title: Remote Registry and CDN References
low_findings.description: The skill references the HyperFrames registry on GitHub and a GSAP CDN script in a demo convention. These are expected project resources, but they create normal supply-chain trust on remote content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible and align with the skill purpose. I found no evidence that credentials or local data are sent to these endpoints.

content.value_statement: HyperFrames users need reliable steps for adding reusable blocks and effects without breaking composition timing. This skill explains install commands, file locations, and host wiring for registry items.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use hyperframes add for blocks and components.
Shows default install paths and hyperframes.json path remapping.
Guides block wiring with composition source, timing, dimensions, and layer attributes.
Guides component merging for HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and GSAP timeline calls.
Describes registry discovery through the manifest and item metadata.
Provides worked examples for adding a chart block and shimmer component.
limitations: Does not provide the HyperFrames CLI implementation itself.
Does not verify remote registry contents before installation.
Requires an existing HyperFrames project for most workflows.
Does not cover example installation beyond pointing to hyperframes init.

use_cases.title: Add a Registry Block
use_cases.description: Install a reusable block and wire it into an existing host timeline with correct timing and dimensions.

use_cases.title: Merge a Visual Effect Component
use_cases.description: Copy component HTML, CSS, and optional JavaScript into a composition while preserving timeline behavior.

use_cases.title: Audit Registry Configuration
use_cases.description: Check registry URLs, install paths, and item metadata before adding shared assets to a project.

prompt_templates.title: Install a Block
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner user adding the first registry block.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me add the data-chart block to my HyperFrames project and explain where the installed file goes.

prompt_templates.title: Wire a Block Into a Timeline
prompt_templates.scenario: User has installed a block and needs host composition guidance.
prompt_templates.prompt: I installed a HyperFrames block. Show me what attributes I need to set in index.html for timing, layer order, and dimensions.

prompt_templates.title: Merge a Component Snippet
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to integrate a component into existing HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide me through adding the shimmer-sweep component to my title scene and connecting it to my GSAP timeline.

prompt_templates.title: Review Registry Setup
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user validating configuration before registry work.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my HyperFrames registry setup, custom install paths, and planned block/component wiring for possible mistakes.

output_examples.input: User asks to add a chart block beside speaker video.
output: Identifies data-chart as a block installed under the configured block path.
Explains the host timeline attributes needed for start time, duration, dimensions, and layer order.
Recommends running lint and preview after wiring.

output_examples.input: User asks how to add a shimmer effect to title text.
output: Explains that shimmer-sweep is a component snippet, not a standalone block.
Lists which HTML, CSS, and JavaScript parts should be merged into the host composition.
Describes where to place the GSAP timeline call.

output_examples.input: User wants to discover available registry items.
output: Points to the registry manifest and item metadata structure.
Separates blocks, components, and examples by type.
Notes that add only installs blocks and components.

best_practices: Run registry commands from the project root or pass an explicit target directory.
Verify composition IDs, timing, duration, dimensions, and track order after adding blocks.
Read each component comment header before merging snippet HTML, styles, and scripts.
anti_patterns: Do not paste component scripts after timeline code when setup must run first.
Do not assume a block path if hyperframes.json remaps install directories.
Do not use hyperframes add for examples; use the init example workflow instead.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps install HyperFrames registry blocks and components, then wire them into existing compositions.

faq.question: Does it install examples?
faq.answer: No. The skill states that examples use the HyperFrames init example workflow instead.

faq.question: What is the difference between blocks and components?
faq.answer: Blocks are standalone sub-compositions. Components are snippets merged into a host composition.

faq.question: Can install paths be changed?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill explains how hyperframes.json remaps block, component, and asset paths.

faq.question: Does this skill execute commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It documents commands for the user or agent to run deliberately in a HyperFrames project.

faq.question: What should users check after wiring a registry item?
faq.answer: They should check composition IDs, file paths, timing, dimensions, layer order, and lint results.
